Name: Tangela
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Even if one of its arms is eaten, it’s fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.

Name: Shuckle
Effects: Heal 40 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its tentacles to lash itself to trees, it lies in wait for prey, luring it close with sweet-smelling drool.

Name: Snivy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

Name: Servine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage. They retaliate with masterful whipping techniques.

Name: Serperior
Ability-Effect: At any times between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Effects: Move as many [G] Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

Name: Sewaddle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it has no Weakness.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

Name: Dwebble
Flavor-Text: When it finds a stone of a suitable size, it secretes a liquid from its mouth to open up a hole to crawl into.

Name: Crustle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Leaf Wallop does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

Name: Genesect
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Attach 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

Name: Charizard
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that Charizard’s fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: If this Pokémon has any basic Energy attached to it that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Each of its nine tails is imbued with supernatural power, and it can live for a thousand years.

Name: Moltres
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can’t use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Effects: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Victini-EX
Effects: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tepig
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

Name: Pignite
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When it trouble, it emits smoke.

Name: Emboar
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Reshiram-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of Prize cards both players have taken.
Flavor-Text: Once it begins to rampage, a Gyarados will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: If this Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

Name: Phione
Effects: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

Name: Oshawott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

Name: Dewott
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another. It never neglects maintaining its scalchops.

Name: Samurott
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

Name: Tympole
Flavor-Text: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. It can turn a boulder to rubble with one punch.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Keldeo-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If Minun is on your Bench, draw 4 more cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Minun
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: They live on treetops and glide using the inside of a cape-like membrane while discharging electricity.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

Name: Zekrom-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Natu
Flavor-Text: It picks food from cactus plants, deftly avoiding buds and spines. It seems to skip about to move.

Name: Xatu
Effects: You and your opponent play Rock-Paper-Scissors. The player who wins draws 3 cards. The player who loses discards the top 3 cards of his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Misdreavus
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.

Name: Woobat
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for 2 [P] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

Name: Sigilyph
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).
Flavor-Text: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

Name: Gothita
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stares intently at everything. It can become so obsessed with watching that it doesn’t notice attacks.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

Name: Gothitelle
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Solosis
Flavor-Text: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others

Name: Solosis
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe.

Name: Duosion
Flavor-Text: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Does 40 damage times the number of Reuniclus you have in play.
Flavor-Text: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

Name: Chandelure-EX
Effects: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

Name: Gallade
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flavor-Text: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

Name: Excadrill-EX
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: Each player can’t play any ACE SPEC cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [D] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [D] Pokémon you have in play.
Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Rayquaza
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

Name: Gabite
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [D] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Discard 2 [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack.

Name: Black Kyurem-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: White Kyurem-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lugia-EX
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can’t discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: For some reason, it likes to land on people’s heads softly and act like it’s a hat.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

Name: Druddigon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Bianca
Description: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Cedric Juniper
Description: Put a Pokémon from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the height of that Pokémon. Reveal that Pokémon. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 3 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 3 cards. Return the Pokémon to your hand. (You can’t choose a Pokémon that doesn’t have the height printed on the card.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

Name: Snivy
Effects: Attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

Name: Servine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it gets dirty, its leaves can’t be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

Name: Serperior
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [G] Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Extremely loyal to its Trainer, it will bark at those who approach the Trainer unexpectedly and run them out of town.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

Name: Piplup
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

Name: Ralts
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Draw a card for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

Name: Gardevoir
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon. This Pokémon can’t use Eternal Radiance during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

Name: Meloetta-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When its opponent can’t be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their cute act is a ruse. They trick people and steal their valuables just to see the looks on their faces.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Put a Teddiursa from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, attach an Energy card from your discard pile to that Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

Name: Audino
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number heads.
Flavor-Text: Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Minccino greet each other by grooming one another thoroughly with their tails.

Name: Cinccino
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Cinccino’s body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

Name: Elesa
Description: Search your deck for 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Shaymin-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It glides on its outstretched membrane while shocking foes with the electricity stored in the pouches on its cheeks.

Name: Mew-EX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meloetta-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
